generated_description: An adversary executed an obfuscated PowerShell command line to bypass User Account Control by hijacking a legitimate Windows binary. This initial execution retrieved and ran a further encoded PowerShell payload from a registry key. The subsequent payload performed defense evasion by disabling PowerShell script block logging, bypassing AMSI, and disabling script signature checks. It then established command and control by making an outbound HTTP connection to an external IP address, using a custom User-Agent and a hardcoded cookie, to download and execute additional encrypted content.
